title: Memory Forgetting & Decay
concept: Implementing decay weights or cleanup tasks to prune low-value, duplicate, or obsolete memories.

exercises.prompt: What should you do when new information conflicts with existing knowledge?

options.text: Update or merge it with the existing knowledge

exercises.explain: The correct answer is to update or merge it with the existing knowledge. This helps keep your memory accurate and relevant.

cards.title: Memory Forgetting is Important
cards.body: Forgetting is not just losing information; it's a process that helps us manage our knowledge effectively.

cards.title: What is Memory Decay?
cards.body: Memory decay refers to the gradual fading or loss of memories over time, especially if they are not used often.

cards.title: Memory Pruning Analogy
cards.body: Think of your memory like a garden. If you don't prune the weeds (unimportant memories), they can overgrow and choke out the flowers (important memories).

cards.title: Example of Memory Decay
cards.body: If you learned a new language but haven't practiced in a year, many words may start to fade from your memory.

cards.title: Myth: You Only Forget Important Memories
cards.body: Many people think they only forget important memories. In fact, unimportant and obsolete memories fade away too!

cards.title: Memory Organization
cards.body: Properly managing memories can help keep your mind clear.

right.text: Weak, old memories take up space.

exercises.prompt: What is a good way to implement memory decay?

options.text: Implement decay weights to remove weak memories
Always add new memories on top of old ones
Never forget anything, no matter what

exercises.explain: Implementing decay weights to remove weak memories helps ensure that only useful information remains, enhancing the efficiency of memory.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Memory decay affects only the memories we deem as unnecessary.
exercises.explain: False: Memory decay affects all memories over time, regardless of their perceived importance.

exercises.prompt: Memory decay happens when memories gradually ________ over time.
exercises.sentence: Memory decay happens when memories gradually fade over time.
exercises.explain: Fade is the correct term because memory decay specifically refers to the gradual loss or weakening of memories.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a method to manage obsolete memories?
exercises.explain: Implementing decay weights helps manage obsolete memories by gradually removing weak ones from your knowledge base.

exercises.prompt: What is the best way to ensure your memory stays sharp and useful?

options.text: Prune weak memories and keep important ones
Only focus on the most recent memories

exercises.explain: Pruning weak memories ensures that your memory is focused on what's important, making it easier to access useful information when you need it. This approach serves as a foundation for efficient memory management, enabling better decision-making for future learning.
